name: Self-Evolution Research
description: Created 4 comprehensive research documents (5,900+ lines total) covering: (1) Prompt A/B testing with multi-armed bandits, (2) ML autoresearch loops with SageMaker/Bedrock, (3) Self-modifying infrastructure with Cedar policies, (4) Autonomous skill generation from sessions. Outputs at docs/research/evolution/.

name: typescript-evolution-engine-modular-design
description: Evolution engine uses 6 separate modules (types, safety-harness, model-router, prompt-optimizer, auto-skill-gen, iac-modifier, experiment-runner) with clear separation: types define interfaces, safety-harness enforces Cedar policies + rate limits, each module handles one evolution dimension

name: bayesian-model-routing-thompson-sampling
description: Bayesian model router uses Thompson Sampling with Beta distributions (alpha/beta parameters) to balance exploration vs exploitation per task category. Each (task_category, model) pair maintains success/failure counts, router samples from Beta distributions to select models, gradually converging to best performers while exploring alternatives

content: All self-evolution operations must pass through EvolutionSafetyHarness which enforces: (1) rate limits (max changes per day/week), (2) Cedar policy authorization checks, (3) immutable safety boundaries (cannot modify safety instructions, audit config, or Cedar policies themselves), (4) cost thresholds requiring human approval

description: MemoryEntry type exported from both memory/types.ts and evolution/types.ts caused TypeScript compilation error (TS2308: Module has already exported a member)
resolution: Renamed evolution module's type to EvolutionMemoryEntry to avoid collision. Check for type name collisions with existing modules before exporting from new modules

name: phase-6-evolution-cdk-lambda-pattern
description: EvolutionStack CDK uses inline Python lambdas with TODO placeholders. Production implementation should move to TypeScript module imports: CDK L3 → state machine → Lambda definitions use code.fromInline() → Handler logic stubs. Lesson: Defer Lambda implementation to builder phase, use inline stubs only for scaffolding.

content: EvolutionSafetyHarness enforces 7-step validation: (1) rate limits (10/day, 3 infra/day, 3 prompts/week), (2) Cedar policy evaluation, (3) cost check ( threshold), (4) S3 snapshot, (5) execute, (6) health check (auto-rollback >10%), (7) audit. Cedar DENY is silent on failure—no exception. All evolution subsystems (PromptOptimizer, ModelRouter, AutoSkillGenerator, IaC) must pass through harness.

Thompson sampling model routing: Beta(alpha, beta) per model per task category. Selection blends quality and cost (1-costSensitivity) * quality + costSensitivity * costFactor. Model pricing hardcoded—refresh needed. Default prior 1.0. State: DynamoDB TENANT#{id}#MODEL_ROUTING.

description: Lambda function bodies: All 8 evolution Lambda functions are TODO stubs with inline Python mock code. Blocks builder phase (40-60 hrs needed). Functions: AnalyzeConversationLogs, GeneratePromptVariant, TestPromptVariant, DetectPatterns, GenerateSkill, MemoryGarbageCollection, ProcessFeedback, RollbackChange.
resolution: Builder sprint week 1-4: implement all 8 Lambda handlers sequentially.

name: self-reflection-feedback-loop
description: ChimeraAgent.performSelfReflection() hook executes after ReAct loop to capture interaction quality (tool success rate, response length, completion status). Stores metadata in memory as system messages for evolution subsystems to aggregate into health scores. Non-blocking - errors logged but don't break agent.

content: EvolutionSafetyHarness validates all self-evolution operations: (1) Rate limits checked first (10/day evolution, 3/day infra, 3/week prompts), (2) Cedar policy evaluation with context (cost delta, human approval, change type), (3) Three validation layers: prompt safety (blocks safety_instructions/content_policy/guardrails/cedar policy/authorization sections), config safety (blocks audit.enabled/audit.trail/guardrails.enabled/cedar.policy_store/evolution.safety_limits), infra safety (blocks delete_table/delete_bucket/modify_iam/modify_vpc/modify_security_group/delete_runtime). Human approval required for cost deltas >= $100/month.

name: health-score-calculation
description: calculateHealthScore() aggregates 7 weighted metrics into 0-100 score: responseQuality (25%), taskCompletion (20%), costEfficiency (15%), correctionRateInv (15%), skillReuse (10%), memoryHitRate (10%), rollbackRateInv (5%). analyzeEvolutionTrends() detects improving/declining/stable trends with confidence based on data points (7 days = 1.0 confidence). shouldThrottleEvolution() implements circuit breaker: pause if health < 40 (critical, 72h), rollback > 30% (high, 48h), or rapid decline > 20 points (high, 48h).

name: phase6-evolution-engine-3builder-implementation
description: 3-builder parallel decomposition for self-evolution engine: Builder A (optimize) prompt A/B + Thompson sampling + experiments, Builder B (generate) N-gram auto-skill + IaC Cedar gating, Builder C (safety) 7-step harness + self-reflection + agent integration. 3854 lines, 144 tests. Safety harness is the integration point for all evolution dimensions.

name: tool-sequence-pattern-detection-ngrams
description: Pattern detection for auto-skill generation: Extract tool sequences from conversation logs, generate n-grams (sliding window of size 3), count frequencies with Counter, filter by min_occurrences threshold (default 5). Store top patterns in DynamoDB with confidence score (count/conversation_count). Pattern format: 'tool1 -> tool2 -> tool3'.

name: tenant-aware-model-routing
description: ModelRouter accepts TenantModelConfig as constructor dependency, making routing decisions based on tenant tier, allowed models, and routing mode. Supports both static (single default model) and auto (Thompson Sampling) modes. Model pool is expandable via availableModelsWithCosts field, allowing runtime model addition without code changes.

content: model-router-expandable-pool: ModelRouter accepts TenantModelConfig with allowedModels + availableModelsWithCosts. ModelId is string (not hardcoded union). DEFAULT_MODEL_COSTS provides fallback for 4 known models. initializeArms() uses filtered pool. loadState() prunes stale arms to allowed models only.

model-routing-mode-toggle: TenantModelConfig.routingMode ('auto'|'static') controls model selection. Static returns defaultModel without Thompson Sampling. Auto uses existing Bayesian routing. Default is 'auto' for backward compat. ModelSelection includes routingMode and explanation string.

tier-based-model-restrictions-at-config: Per-tenant model restrictions enforced at TenantModelConfig creation time (tenant-service.ts), not at ModelRouter runtime. Tiers control which models appear in allowedModels: basic=2 models (nova-lite,sonnet), advanced=3 (add opus), enterprise/dedicated=4 (all). Router just uses allowedModels without tier awareness.

name: dgm-inspired-evolution-loop
description: DGM-inspired evolution loop for Chimera: archive of agent configs (prompts, model weights, skills) with fitness scores and lineage -> diversity-aware parent selection -> LLM mutation (meta-agent generates variants) -> Cedar safety harness -> sandbox eval against golden dataset -> A/B deployment. Key: keep suboptimal variants as stepping stones (dont discard A/B losers), use composite fitness (quality + cost + latency + safety), track parent-child lineage to analyze productive evolutionary paths.

content: generateSkill Lambda uses pattern-based SKILL.md synthesis: filter top_patterns by confidence >= 0.7 and count >= 3, derive kebab skill name from tool sequence, generate structured SKILL.md, store in S3 under skills/{tenant_id}/{skill_name}/SKILL.md, record DynamoDB metadata with PENDING_REVIEW status.

evolution-lambda-stub-completion: All 5 TODO stub handlers in evolution-stack.ts replaced with real boto3/DynamoDB logic. analyzeConversationLogs queries TENANT#tid#LOGS range; generatePromptVariant reads ACTIVE prompt and applies rule-based improvements; testPromptVariant loads golden-datasets from S3 and scores keyword overlap; memoryGarbageCollection transitions lifecycle via GSI1-lifecycle (active->warm->cold->archived) per ADR-016; processFeedback routes thumbs_down/up/correction/remember via GSI2-unprocessed-feedback. Functions needing env vars (generatePromptVariant, testPromptVariant, memoryGC, processFeedback) had environment blocks added alongside existing grants.

self-evolution-orchestrator-flow: SelfEvolutionOrchestrator in packages/core/src/evolution/ wires CDKGenerator+CodeCommitWorkspaceManager+CodePipelineDeployer+EvolutionSafetyHarness into 8-step authorize→generate→validate→commit→trigger→register→audit flow. Static CDK validator blocks DESTROY policy, IAM mutations, VPC/SG changes, and code >64KB. Capability registration writes to evolution state table under CAPABILITY# SK.
